title: lappa bardana var. major
intro: Lappa bardana var. major, also known as Greater Burdock, is a biennial or perennial plant in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Greater Burdock include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and skin conditions. Greater Burdock can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to treat skin issues such as eczema and acne.
